meta.description: Soul trait pairs. Every person holds exactly 3 traits, one from each of 3 randomly chosen pairs. A person cannot hold both sides of any pair. sideAWeight is the probability (0.0-1.0) that side A is picked — reflects real-world distribution where some traits are more common than their opposite.

sideA.description: Steps toward danger rather than away. Does not freeze under pressure or in unfamiliar situations.

sideB.description: Pulls back when the stakes rise. Will find reasons to avoid confrontation, risk, or exposure.

sideA.description: Stays level in chaos. Crisis does not distort their thinking or their body.

sideB.description: Pressure scrambles them. Small crises become large ones when they are involved.

sideA.description: Sees themselves accurately. Takes correction without ego. Credits others freely.

sideB.description: Overestimates themselves, underestimates others. Resists feedback. Needs to be the story.

sideA.description: Willing to wait for the right moment. Does not force outcomes. Trusts process over shortcuts.

sideB.description: Needs to see results now. Cuts corners when progress feels slow. Frustrates coaches and sparring partners.

sideA.description: Extends good faith. Believes people are working with them, not against them, until shown otherwise.

sideB.description: Reads threat into neutral events. Assumes ulterior motives. Hard to build a team around.

sideA.description: Keeps to the plan. Shows up, does the work, respects the structure. Reliable under accumulation.

sideB.description: Acts before thinking. Takes shortcuts that cost them later. Exciting to watch, expensive to manage.

sideA.description: Keeps going after setbacks. Treats failure as information. Does not quit in the dark.

sideB.description: Loses shape when things go wrong. A bad loss, a harsh word, or a cold streak can break their momentum for weeks.

sideA.description: Always wants more. Never fully satisfied with where they are. Drives themselves past comfort.

sideB.description: Settles at good enough. Does not burn for more. Hard to push past a comfortable plateau.
